provenance.source_corpus: Captive-reared burrowing owls show higher site-affinity, survival, and reproductive performance when reintroduced using a soft-release

source_spans.text: soft-releases were performed in the same manner as concurrent hard-releases
soft-released burrowing owls were more likely to stay in release areas than were hard-released owls
Breeding-season survival was 70% for soft-released owls vs. 50% for hard-released owls
On average, soft-released owls produced 50% more fledglings than concurrently hard-released owls
0.17 offspring were recruited into the local wild breeding population per soft-released pair, compared to 0.05 local recruits per hard-released pair
higher proportion of all deaths of hard-released owls being attributed to avian predators... than were for soft-released owls
